repo_prior_art.cite: none; ThmSmith PartialID catalogue is seed-only for this cluster

published_prior_art.cite: JessonDouglasManshausenSolalMeinshausenStierGalShalit2022 and MarmarelisHaddadJessonJahanshadGalstyanVerSteeg2023 give continuous-dose sensitivity bounds, not finite-grid monotone-concave joint pasting
Manski1997 concave-monotone response already gives chord tightening logic; novelty depends on Conjecture 1's CMSM sharpness
Imbens2000/KennedyMaMcHughSmall2017 point-identification benchmark for continuous dose response under unconfoundedness

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: Baitairian et al. 2025, 'Sharp Bounds for Continuous-Valued Treatment Effects with Unobserved Confounders', is a closer pointwise-sharp continuous sensitivity comparator and is missing from the checklist.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The class W_{Gamma,j}(P) is not pinned by an exact CMSM density-ratio inequality; the proposal must state the ratio orientation and conditioning objects before a normalization-pasting claim is well-defined.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add Baitairian et al. 2025 as the pointwise sharp continuous-sensitivity comparator and explain why its sharp APO bounds do not imply finite-grid shape/joint-pasting sharpness.
Replace the informal W_{Gamma,j} definition with the exact Jesson CMSM inequality specialized to each grid dose, including whether the ratio conditions on Y(a_j), U, or both.
Add a small counterexample-or-construction paragraph showing how two grid-dose tilts would be pasted through a common latent partition in the K=2 case.

recommended_next_step: Patch the CMSM definition and literature comparison, then re-run Stage -0.5; the field-level kernel survives if those edits are clean.
